evals.prompt: 我想在香蕉派上对 ResNet50 做 perf profiling，用 generic_ort_runner 跑 30 次迭代，采样 999Hz。板子 IP 是 192.168.1.22，密码 bianbu。
evals.expected_output: Agent follows the SKILL workflow step by step: (1) checks local artifacts (runner, rootfs.tar.gz, model), downloads model if missing; (2) SSH to board via sshpass, checks perf and kernel.perf_event_paranoid; (3) scp uploads rootfs.tar.gz, extracts, copies model into rootfs; (4) mounts /proc /sys /dev into rootfs; (5) runs perf stat -d / perf record -e cpu-clock -g -F 999 / perf report --symfs / perf annotate via chroot; (6) scp downloads results to output/perf/resnet50/; (7) cleans up remote temp files; (8) generates summary.md. Auto-detects framework as ORT from .onnx extension and uses chroot mode.

evals.prompt: 帮我对 ResNet50、MobileNetV2 两个模型做批量 perf profiling，对比热点函数。香蕉派 192.168.1.22，密码 bianbu。
evals.expected_output: Agent uploads rootfs once, then loops through models: scp model into rootfs → perf stat/record/report/annotate → scp download results → cleanup perf temp files. Parses perf_stat.txt for cycles/IPC/cache-miss, parses perf_report.txt for top functions. Generates summary.md with comparison table. Identifies shared hot functions across models as priority fusion targets. Notes cpu-clock event is used for RISC-V SBI PMU.

evals.prompt: 帮我对 Qwen2.5-0.5B-Q4_0.gguf 用 llama.cpp 做 perf profiling，prompt 是 'Hello world'，生成 128 tokens。香蕉派 192.168.1.22。
evals.expected_output: Agent follows the direct-mode workflow: (1) checks local llama-cli, lib/*.so, model; (2) SSH checks board; (3) scp uploads llama-cli, lib/*.so, model; (4) runs perf with LD_LIBRARY_PATH set: perf record -e cpu-clock -g -F 999 -- LD_LIBRARY_PATH=<remote>/lib <remote>/llama-cli -m <model>.gguf -p 'Hello world' -n 128; (5) scp downloads results to output/perf/qwen-0.5b/; (6) generates summary. Auto-detects framework as llama from .gguf extension. Notes longer timeout needed for LLM inference.

evals.prompt: 我从 perf annotate 里看到一段热点汇编，连续的 ld + addw + sw 占了 30% 的采样，这是什么类型的 fusion 候选？还有 mulw + addw 的链式组合占了 25%，这两种哪个收益更大？
evals.expected_output: Agent identifies: (1) ld+addw+sw as load-compute-store (memory-ALU fusion) pattern, (2) mulw+addw as multiply-accumulate chain (MAC fusion) pattern. Explains both are high-value candidates. Compares: MAC fusion likely has higher benefit because multiply has longer latency and MAC is common in CNN kernels. Recommends profiling more models to see if both patterns appear consistently across workloads.
